steps.title: Substitute x = 3 into the function
steps.content: To find $h(3)$, substitute $x = 3$ into the function definition: $$h(3) = 14 \cdot 2^{-3} + 7$$

steps.title: Evaluate the negative exponent
steps.content:
Negative exponents represent reciprocals: $2^{-3} = \frac{1}{2^3} = \frac{1}{8}$.

Substitute this value: $$h(3) = 14 \cdot \frac{1}{8} + 7 = \frac{14}{8} + 7$$

steps.title: Simplify fractions and combine
steps.content:
Reduce $\frac{14}{8}$ to $\frac{7}{4}$, then convert \$7$ to fourths: $7 = \frac{28}{4}$.

$$h(3) = \frac{7}{4} + \frac{28}{4} = \frac{35}{4}$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
